raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "classifying and labeling aversive or harmful mental states as characteristic of anger",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "aversion to a person or desires that tend toward their harm constitute a distinct category of soul-state",
    "such states are equivalent to or exemplified by angry souls",
    "these states are identifiable and worth distinguishing (as signaled by 'Secondly')"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as a descriptive taxonomy but lacks any explicit action, criterion, or behavioral directive, weakening its status as a commitment",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the reader already accepts the underlying Stoic psychology and uses the description as a diagnostic cue to interrupt their own aversive impulses",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the passage is treated as a complete behavioral rule rather than an incomplete fragment, or is applied to justify labeling others' motives without self-examination"
}
